trade.summary: Modest bilateral trade. Brasile exports soybeans, iron ore, and chemicals to Malesia. Malesia exports palm oil, electronics, rubber, and liquefied natural gas to Brasile.

diplomatic.summary: Normal diplomatic relations. Both countries cooperate in G20 and multilateral forums. Shared interest in tropical forest conservation despite competition in palm oil vs. soy markets. Both pursue strategic autonomy.

military.summary: Very limited military ties. No alliance. Minimal defense cooperation.

financial.summary: Limited bilateral investment. Petronas has explored Brasileian energy markets. Some Malesian conglomerates have modest Brasileian presence.

energy.summary: Malesia exports LNG to Brasile. Some cooperation on bioenergy. Limited direct energy partnership.

scientific.summary: Limited scientific collaboration. Some joint research in tropical agriculture, forestry, and biodiversity.
